title: potentilla tabernaemontani subvar. quinata
intro: Potentilla tabernaemontani subvar. quinata, also known as the five-leaf potentilla, is a plant species within the rose family and is native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. The roots of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The health benefits of Potentilla tabernaemontani subvar. quinata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of the common cold and flu, and soothe digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made by infusing the roots in hot water to create a tea or decoction, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
